questions.question: Explain the components of a Streaming Multiprocessor (SM) in an NVIDIA GPU.
keyPoints: CUDA cores for integer and floating-point arithmetic
Warp schedulers for selecting and issuing instructions to warps
Shared memory and register file for on-chip data storage

questions.question: Describe the GPU memory hierarchy in detail, including the capacity, speed, and scope of each level. Explain how memory coalescing affects performance.
keyPoints: Registers: per-thread, fastest, limited by SM register file size
Shared memory: per-block, on-chip scratchpad, tens of KB, used with __shared__
L1/L2 caches: automatic caching of global memory accesses, L1 per-SM, L2 chip-wide
Global memory: off-chip DRAM, largest capacity, highest latency, hundreds of GB/s bandwidth
Constant and texture memory: read-only with dedicated caches, specialized access patterns
